description:
You are an automation-workflow engineer who needs every project to run in the same
    regional context no matter which server the container ends up on.
    
    Goal
    ----
    1. Define a reusable, project-local time-zone & locale configuration.
    2. Create a small helper script that loads those settings and writes a
       deterministic, single-line timestamp into a log file.
    3. Execute the helper script once so the log is present for later CI checks.
    
    Directory layout you must end up with
    -------------------------------------
    /home/user/workflow/                            (new directory)
    ├── config/                                     (new directory)
    │   └── regional_settings.sh                    (new file – *see exact contents below*)
    ├── scripts/                                    (new directory)
    │   └── generate_timestamp.sh                   (new file – *see exact contents below*)
    └── logs/                                       (new directory)
        └── time_check.log                          (created **by** the script)
    
    Exact contents that must be created
    -----------------------------------
    
    1. /home/user/workflow/config/regional_settings.sh
       ------------------------------------------------
       # Regional settings for automation workflow
       export TZ="America/New_York"
       export LC_ALL="de_DE.UTF-8"
    
       (There must be *no* extra commands or blank lines after the two exports.)
    
    2. /home/user/workflow/scripts/generate_timestamp.sh
       -------------------------------------------------
       #!/usr/bin/env bash
       # Generate a fixed, reproducible timestamp in the configured TZ/locale
       source /home/user/workflow/config/regional_settings.sh
       date -d '2024-10-05 14:45:30 UTC' '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S %Z' \
         > /home/user/workflow/logs/time_check.log
    
       Remarks:
       • Do not write anything to stdout/stderr except what date already prints
         to the redirected file.
       • Make sure the script is executable.
    
    3. /home/user/workflow/logs/time_check.log  
       ----------------------------------------
       The script itself must create this file.  
       After you run the script once, the file must contain *exactly* (including
       the trailing newline):
    
       2024-10-05 10:45:30 EDT
    
    Success criteria (this is what automated tests will check)
    ----------------------------------------------------------
    • All three new sub-directories (config, scripts, logs) exist inside
      /home/user/workflow.
    • /home/user/workflow/config/regional_settings.sh has the **exact** two-line
      body shown above (final newline optional).
    • /home/user/workflow/scripts/generate_timestamp.sh is present, executable
      (mode 755 or equivalent), and its body matches the block shown above
      byte-for-byte (final newline optional).
    • /home/user/workflow/logs/time_check.log exists and its sole content is
    
          2024-10-05 10:45:30 EDT\n
    
      (i.e., “EDT” followed by a single Unix newline, nothing else).
    
    Hint (do not paste directly):
    -----------------------------
    • You will need roughly 5-10 shell commands:
      – mkdir (×3)  
      – cat or printf redirects (×2)  
      – chmod (×1)  
      – bash execution of the new script (×1)
    • You do **not** need root privileges: environment variables are set
      per-process via the sourced file; no system-wide locale generation is
      required.
truth:
Expected files & contents for verification
    ------------------------------------------
    1. File: /home/user/workflow/config/regional_settings.sh  
       Content (exact, with a trailing newline):
    
           # Regional settings for automation workflow
           export TZ="America/New_York"
           export LC_ALL="de_DE.UTF-8"
    
    2. File: /home/user/workflow/scripts/generate_timestamp.sh  
       Content (exact, with a trailing newline after the last line):
    
           #!/usr/bin/env bash
           # Generate a fixed, reproducible timestamp in the configured TZ/locale
           source /home/user/workflow/config/regional_settings.sh
           date -d '2024-10-05 14:45:30 UTC' '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S %Z' \
             > /home/user/workflow/logs/time_check.log
    
    3. The helper script must be executable: mode 755 (u+rwx,go+rx).
    
    4. File: /home/user/workflow/logs/time_check.log  
       Exact content (including newline at end of file):
    
           2024-10-05 10:45:30 EDT
    
    5. Directory structure must match exactly what is listed in the public task.
